original_query: select c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice, sum(l_quantity) from customer, orders, lineitem where o_orderkey in ( select l_orderkey from lineitem group by l_orderkey having sum(l_quantity) > 315 ) and c_custkey = o_custkey and o_orderkey = l_orderkey group by c_name, c_custkey, o_orderkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice order by o_totalprice desc, o_orderdate limit 100;
rewritten_query:
SELECT
    c.c_name,
    c.c_custkey,
    o.o_orderkey,
    o.o_orderdate,
    o.o_totalprice,
    fo.l_sum
FROM
    customer c
    JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey
    JOIN (
        SELECT
            l_orderkey,
            SUM(l_quantity) AS l_sum
        FROM
            lineitem
        GROUP BY
            l_orderkey
        HAVING
            SUM(l_quantity) > 315
    ) fo ON o.o_orderkey = fo.l_orderkey
ORDER BY
    o.o_totalprice DESC,
    o.o_orderdate
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Converted the IN subquery to a JOIN with a derived table that includes the precomputed sum
Eliminated redundant scan of lineitem table by using the precomputed sum from the subquery
Removed unnecessary GROUP BY clause since each order will have only one row after optimization
Used explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins in the WHERE clause for better readability and optimization
